page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 virtual CPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6870.73 and a Passmark CPU score of 7626.07. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1304.38 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1155.0. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 382.09 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 51.32 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, perform at an average level of 6.38 million operations per second. However, complex tasks like LLM inference with a 7B model show poor performance, generating only 6.2 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5dn.2xlarge provides a balanced resource density for memory-bound workloads that require fast local scratch space, thanks to its bundled NVMe SSD and high memory allocation. The instance is well-suited for database caching layers, in-memory databases, data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. It is less suitable for compute-heavy workloads, single-threaded processing, or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring local storage. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.1 GHz clock speed. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6870.73 and a cached read memory bandwidth of 382.09 GB/sec. However, single-core processing and complex tasks like LLM inference are relatively weak. This instance is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing applications that benefit from high memory density and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 300 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for in-memory databases and data caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized instance featuring 64 GB DDR4 RAM and 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance with dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR4 RAM, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
300 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suitable for in-memory databases, caching, and data analytics
